description: A 600-year-old UNESCO World Heritage village of the Pungsan Ryu clan set on a bend of the Nakdong River, with preserved thatched and tiled traditional houses. Famous for its mask dance and the Seonyu Julbul Nori firework festival.
gettingThere: From Seoul, take the KTX-Eum from Cheongnyangni Station to Andong Station (~2h10m), then Bus #46 or the Hahoe shuttle from Andong (~40-60 min). ~3.5-4 hours total.
nearby: Hahoe Mask Museum at the village entrance
bestTime: Autumn for foliage, or evenings during the Seonyu Julbul Nori firework festival; weekday mornings are quietest.
photoTip: Cross the river by ferry to Buyongdae Cliff for the postcard panorama of the whole village wrapped by the Nakdong River.
whyFamous: A UNESCO village that doubles as a live-action backdrop for period dramas; the river-and-village panorama is instantly recognisable.

titles.titleEn: Detective K: Secret of the Lost Island

description: A 64-meter cliff facing Hahoe Village across the Nakdong River, offering the classic panoramic view of the village. A longtime favorite backdrop for historical films and dramas.
gettingThere: From Seoul, take the KTX-Eum from Cheongnyangni to Andong Station (~2h10m), then bus/shuttle to Hahoe; the cliff is across the river from the village via a walking trail. ~4 hours total.
nearby: Okyeonjeongsa pavilion at the cliff base

description: A scenic Joseon-era Confucian academy (a UNESCO World Heritage 'Seowon' site) overlooking the Nakdong River, celebrated for its Mandaeru pavilion and considered one of Korea's most beautiful seowon.
gettingThere: From Seoul, take the KTX-Eum from Cheongnyangni to Andong Station (~2h10m); from Hahoe Village it is ~10 min by taxi via a narrow road, or a seasonal shuttle. ~4 hours total.

nameEn: Andong Seonghee Girls' High School (Hyosan High School)
description: A real Andong high school whose riverside campus served as the exterior of 'Hyosan High School' in the global hit zombie series. Note it is a functioning school, so view from outside and respect class hours.
gettingThere: From Seoul, take the KTX-Eum from Cheongnyangni to Andong Station (~2h10m), then a short local taxi or bus within Andong city. ~2.5-3 hours total.
nearby: Andong city center and Nakdong River walks
bestTime: Weekends or after school hours — it's a working school, so avoid disrupting students.
photoTip: Frame the school building exterior from the street, the way the show's establishing shots did — stay outside the fence.
whyFamous: The 2022 Netflix #1 zombie hit 'All of Us Are Dead' made this ordinary riverside school globally recognisable overnight.

description: Korea's largest historical-drama (sageuk) backlot: a 70,000-square-meter recreated Joseon-era palace-and-town complex of about 130 buildings inside Mungyeong Saejae Provincial Park. Visitors can try on royal costumes.
gettingThere: No direct KTX to Mungyeong town. Take an intercity bus from Dong-Seoul Bus Terminal to Mungyeong (~2h), then a local taxi/bus to Mungyeong Saejae (~15 min). ~2.5-3 hours total.
nearby: Mungyeong Rail Bike along the Yeonggang River
Cafe Gaeun Station (a converted disused railway station)
bestTime: Spring and autumn for mild weather; arrive early to shoot the palace gates before tour groups fill the courtyards.
photoTip: Rent a royal hanbok at the entrance and frame yourself against the main palace gate or throne hall — the exact backdrops used in the sageuk dramas.
whyFamous: As Korea's biggest sageuk backlot, almost every famous historical drama (Kingdom, The Red Sleeve, Under the Queen's Umbrella) has shot here — fans come to walk the actual palace sets.

description: Korea's largest open film set recreating 1950s-80s Korea across three zones: a 1960s Suncheon township, a 1970s Seoul hillside shantytown, and a 1980s suburban street. Built in 2006 for the drama 'Love and Ambition.'
gettingThere: From Seoul, take the KTX from Yongsan Station to Suncheon Station (~2.5-3h), then a local taxi (~10 min) or city bus to the set. ~3-3.5 hours total.
nearby: Suncheon Bay National Garden (Korea's first national garden)

titles.titleEn: The Legend of the Blue Sea

description: Korea's most famous tourist tea plantation, founded in 1939, with rows of emerald terraced tea bushes climbing a hillside lined with towering cedar trees. Its cedar-lined entrance path is one of the most-photographed scenes in Korean media.
gettingThere: From Seoul, take the KTX to Gwangju-Songjeong Station (~1h40m), transfer to U-Square Terminal for an intercity bus to Boseong, then a ~15-min taxi to the plantation; or a direct express bus from Seoul Central City Terminal (~5.5h). ~4-5.5 hours total.
nearby: Yulpo Beach with a green-tea seawater spa (~10 km)
bestTime: Late April to June, when the tea bushes are vivid green; early morning avoids crowds and harsh midday glare.
photoTip: The signature shot is the towering cedar-lined entrance path, framed straight down the avenue; climb the hillside paths for the layered terraced-tea panorama.
whyFamous: Decades of dramas, films and ads have made its cedar-lined tea path the default 'green Korea' image — it trends every spring.

description: A hillside village of red-roofed German-style houses built from the early 2000s by Korean 'Gastarbeiter' miners and nurses who worked in Germany in the 1960s-70s and returned home. It overlooks Mulgeon Bay and hosts an autumn Oktoberfest.
gettingThere: No KTX serves Namhae. Take an intercity bus from Seoul Nambu (or Central City) Terminal to Namhae Bus Terminal (~4.5h), then the local Namhae-Mijo bus or a ~15-20 min taxi to the village. ~5 hours total.
nearby: Mulgeon Coastal Forest (a protected old-growth windbreak forest)
German restaurants and craft-beer pubs in the village

description: A dramatic coastal hamlet where over 100 stone-walled terraced rice paddies cascade down a steep slope from Seolheulsan Mountain straight to the sea. A designated Scenic Site with a 1-hour walking loop.
gettingThere: No KTX serves Namhae. Take an intercity bus from Seoul to Namhae Bus Terminal (~4.5h), then a local Nam-myeon bus or a ~25-30 min taxi. ~5 hours total.
nearby: Boriam Hermitage on Geumsan Mountain (a famous sea-view prayer site)
Local cafes and makgeolli houses serving seafood

titles.titleEn: Goblin (Guardian: The Lonely and Great God)

description: A curved stone jetty extending into the sea at Jumunjin Beach in northern Gangneung, site of the iconic scene where Ji Eun-tak summons the goblin by blowing out birthday candles. Fans recreate the scene with a red scarf and buckwheat flowers.
gettingThere: From Seoul, take the KTX from Seoul or Cheongnyangni Station to Gangneung Station (~1h50m), then local bus 302-1 toward Jumunjin (~50 min) or a taxi (~20 min). ~3 hours total.
bestTime: Sunrise — this is one of the east coast's most famous sunrise spots, and the soft morning light matches the drama's mood.
photoTip: Stand on the breakwater facing the sea and recreate the famous 'Goblin' scene — fans bring a red scarf and a small bouquet of buckwheat flowers for the same frame.
whyFamous: It exploded after the 2016 megahit 'Goblin' — the candle-blowing scene shot here became one of K-drama's most recreated moments.

description: A recreated single-story photo studio that was the main setting of the classic melodrama 'Christmas in August.' Rebuilt by Gunsan City as a free attraction displaying original props; staff will email visitors their photos.
gettingThere: No direct KTX to Gunsan. Take a Saemaeul/Mugunghwa train from Yongsan Station to Gunsan Station (~3h-3h20m), then a local bus or taxi (~15-20 min) to the old town. ~3.5 hours total.

description: A well-preserved two-story Japanese colonial-era timber house with original interiors and a Japanese garden, designated National Registered Cultural Heritage in 2005. Frequently used for period and gangster films.
gettingThere: No direct KTX to Gunsan. Take a Saemaeul/Mugunghwa train from Yongsan to Gunsan Station (~3h), then a taxi/bus (~15 min); it sits within walking distance of Chowon Photo Studio. ~3.5 hours total.
